mnemonics.title: Node Structure Components
mnemonics.content: Remember: DATA + NEXT = DANCE. D for Data, N for Next, C for Contains, E for Everything. Each node DANCES through the list.

mnemonics.title: Insertion at Beginning
mnemonics.content: NEW FIRST THEN SHIFT: NewNode->Next = Head, then Head = NewNode. Think 'NEW FIRST' - new node comes first.

mnemonics.title: Deletion Steps
mnemonics.content: SAVE THEN STEAL: Save current head in temp, Move head to next, Free temp. Like saving yourself before stealing the list away.

mnemonics.title: NULL Significance
mnemonics.content: NULL = NO MORE NODES. Think of NULL as the 'EXIT' door of the list - once you reach it, you're done.

acronyms.expansion: Linked List - linear data structure with nodes containing data and pointers
acronyms.usage: Used when size is unknown and dynamic operations are frequent

acronyms.expansion: Singly Linked List - nodes have only forward link
acronyms.usage: Used for simple list operations without needing to traverse backwards

acronyms.expansion: Memory ALLOCation - allocates specified bytes from heap
acronyms.usage: Used to dynamically allocate memory for new nodes: ptr = (struct node*)malloc(sizeof(struct node))

acronyms.expansion: HEAD - pointer to first node in the list
acronyms.usage: Starting point for all traversals and operations in the list
Used to release memory of deleted nodes to prevent memory leaks

visualTips.visualization: Draw each node as a box divided into two sections: LEFT for DATA (labeled with type like int), RIGHT for NEXT (arrow pointing to next box). The last arrow points to NULL written in a small crossed box.
Imagine pulling a new book from a stack and placing it on TOP - the new node becomes the new HEAD, and its NEXT points to where HEAD was pointing.
Array looks like connected train cars (contiguous), Linked List looks like treasure hunt clues scattered around - each clue (node) points to the next location.
Think of following a treasure map - start at HEAD, read data, follow the arrow (NEXT) to the next clue, repeat until you find the EXIT (NULL).

analogies.analogy: Like a train car - each car has passengers (DATA) and a connector to the next car (NEXT pointer). The last car has no connector (NULL).
Like a hotel that can add or remove rooms as needed, unlike an apartment building with fixed rooms (array).
Like reading a chain letter or following a family tree down the paternal line - you must start from the beginning and go through each person in sequence.
Like adding a new page to a ring binder - you just punch a new hole and connect the ring, unlike inserting in the middle of a stapled document (array) which requires moving all pages.
Like the 'END' sign at the last stop on a bus route - it tells the driver (program) there are no more passengers (nodes) to pick up.
